channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) 2026–2027: Fully Funded Continental Youth Leadership Programme | 50 Positions Available Across Africa
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) is now open for applications for the 2026–2027 cohort. This prestigious, fully funded programme offers young African professionals the opportunity to serve as volunteers across the continent while gaining hands-on experience in policy, development, and leadership. If you are passionate about Africa’s development and want to contribute to […]

The post African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) 2026–2027: Fully Funded Continental Youth Leadership Programme | 50 Positions Available Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) is now open for applications for the 2026–2027 cohort. This prestigious, fully funded programme offers young African professionals the opportunity to serve as volunteers across the continent while gaining hands-on experience in policy, development, and leadership.

If you are passionate about Africa’s development and want to contribute to Agenda 2063: “The Africa We Want”, this is one of the most competitive and impactful youth opportunities available.

About the African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC)

Established in 2010, the AU-YVC is a flagship youth leadership initiative designed to:

  • Recruit, train, and deploy young African professionals
  • Promote youth participation in continental development
  • Strengthen skills, leadership, and cross-cultural collaboration

Volunteers are deployed for 12 months in another AU Member State, contributing to development programmes and policy initiatives.

Programme Overview

  • Programme: African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC)
  • Organization: African Union Commission
  • Duration: 12 months
  • Number of Positions: 50
  • Start Date: 2026/2027 cycle
  • Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EAT)

Key Objectives

The AU-YVC programme aims to:

  • Empower young Africans as drivers of development
  • Promote volunteerism across the continent
  • Enhance youth engagement in policy and governance
  • Foster a more integrated, peaceful, and prosperous Africa

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must:

  • Be a citizen of an AU Member State (living in Africa or the diaspora)
  • Be aged 18–35 years
  • Hold at least a post-secondary qualification (TVET, Bachelor’s, Master’s, etc.)
  • Be available for 12 months of full-time volunteer service
  • Be willing to live and work in another African country
  • Be proficient in at least one AU working language (Arabic, English, French, Kiswahili, Portuguese, or Spanish)
  • Have:
    • At least 1 year of volunteering experience
    • At least 1 year of professional work experience

Required Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong computer skills (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
  • Proficiency in email and internet tools
  • Excellent communication (written and verbal)
  • Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills
  • Multilingual ability (an added advantage)

Core Competencies

Successful candidates are expected to:

  • Communicate clearly and effectively
  • Write concisely and professionally
  • Work in multicultural environments
  • Demonstrate integrity and high ethical standards
  • Show commitment and professionalism

Volunteer Benefits and Entitlements

Selected AU Youth Volunteers will receive:

  • Full pre-deployment training
  • Economy return ticket to duty station
  • Monthly stipend for living expenses
  • Health insurance coverage
  • AU Service Passport
  • Separation allowance upon completion
  • Access to training and mentorship opportunities
  • Opportunity to engage in high-level continental policy work
  • Networking with young leaders across Africa

Application Process (Step-by-Step)

1. Complete Your Profile

Visit the AU careers portal and:

  • Register or log in
  • Fill in your personal information

2. Upload Required Documents

  • Updated CV (maximum 3 pages)
  • Passport bio page or national ID
  • Highest qualification certificate (no transcripts)
  • Recommendation letter (not older than 2 years)

3. Submit a Cover Letter

Your cover letter must answer:

  • Why do you want to become an AU Youth Volunteer?
  • What makes you the best candidate?
  • What skills will you bring to the programme?

4. Submit Before Deadline

Ensure your application is submitted by:
April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EAT)

⚠️ Mandatory Requirement: AU CV Template

All applicants must use the official African Union CV template.

  • Applications without the AU CV format will NOT be considered
  • Download, complete, and upload the template before applying

Important Notes

  • Incomplete or late applications will not be accepted
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted
  • Providing false or incomplete information may lead to disqualification
  • The African Union does NOT charge any application or facilitation fees

Why You Should Apply

The AU-YVC is more than a volunteer programme—it is a career-defining opportunity to:

  • Gain continental exposure
  • Build leadership and professional skills
  • Contribute directly to Africa’s development agenda
  • Join a network of future African leaders

The African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) is one of the most impactful youth programmes in Africa, offering a unique platform for young professionals to drive change at a continental level.

If you meet the criteria and are ready to make a difference, apply before the deadline and take your place among Africa’s next generation of leaders.

FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY HERE 

More opportunities for Africans: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Africa

The post African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) 2026–2027: Fully Funded Continental Youth Leadership Programme | 50 Positions Available Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Knauss Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Ocean Policy Program in the USA
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Knauss Fellowship is a prestigious, one-year program that offers graduate students the opportunity to apply their academic training to real-world policy challenges related to oceans, coasts, and the Great Lakes. Hosted by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) through its Sea Grant program, this fellowship places participants in high-impact roles within the U.S. […]

The post Apply for the Knauss Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Ocean Policy Program in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Knauss Fellowship is a prestigious, one-year program that offers graduate students the opportunity to apply their academic training to real-world policy challenges related to oceans, coasts, and the Great Lakes. Hosted by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) through its Sea Grant program, this fellowship places participants in high-impact roles within the U.S. federal government.

Fellows gain hands-on experience in policy-making while contributing scientific knowledge, innovation, and fresh perspectives to national decision-making processes. In return, they develop practical skills, expand their professional networks, and prepare for leadership careers at the intersection of science and public policy.

Fellowship Overview

The Knauss Fellowship provides a unique pathway from academia to government, allowing fellows to engage directly with policy development and implementation.

Duration

One year (June 2027 – May 2028)

Location

Washington, D.C. metropolitan area

Placement Opportunities

Fellows are placed in:

  • Federal executive branch offices
  • Legislative branch offices (e.g., Congress)

Areas of Work

Participants contribute to a wide range of policy-related activities, including:

  • Policy analysis and development
  • Science communication
  • Program evaluation
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Environmental and marine resource management

Application Details

  • Application Status: Open
  • Deadline: June 3, 2026
  • Application Process:
    Applicants must apply through their state Sea Grant program, which reviews submissions and nominates candidates for the national competition.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Knauss Fellowship, applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in a graduate program (Master’s, PhD, or JD) at an accredited university in the United States or its territories at the time of application
  • Demonstrate a strong interest in public service and marine or coastal policy
  • Be a U.S. citizen, national, or dual citizen

Fellowship Impact and Reach

The Knauss Fellowship has a long-standing track record of developing future leaders in marine policy and public service:

  • 47 years of program success
  • 1,762 fellows placed in government roles
  • 69 host offices across the U.S. government (average between 2022–2025)

This extensive reach highlights the fellowship’s role in shaping policy professionals and advancing science-informed decision-making.

Opportunities for Different Participants

Prospective Fellows

Graduate students interested in marine and environmental policy can apply through their state Sea Grant programs and compete for national placement.

Current Fellows & Alumni

Participants gain access to essential career resources and a strong alumni network that supports long-term professional growth.

Prospective Hosts

Government offices interested in hosting fellows in future cycles (e.g., 2027) will have opportunities to participate and benefit from the program’s talent pool.

Recent Knauss Fellowship Updates

Recent announcements from the Sea Grant program highlight the continued growth and competitiveness of the fellowship:

  • 2026: Announcement of the latest fellowship cohorts selected for national programs
  • 2024–2025: Finalists and fellows recognized for their contributions to marine policy and leadership development

These updates reflect the program’s ongoing commitment to bridging science and policy through practical, government-based experience.

Why Apply for the Knauss Fellowship?

The Knauss Fellowship stands out as a premier opportunity for graduate students seeking careers in environmental policy and public service. It offers:

  • Direct exposure to federal policy-making
  • Hands-on experience in executive and legislative offices
  • Opportunities to influence real-world environmental decisions
  • A strong national professional network
  • Career advancement in science-policy leadership

How to Apply

To begin your application:

  1. Locate your state Sea Grant program
  2. Review the Notice of Funding Opportunity
  3. Submit your application through your state program before the deadline

The Knauss Fellowship is more than a fellowship—it is a career-launching platform for future leaders committed to shaping policies that protect and sustain marine and coastal ecosystems.

Discover more similar opportunities here.

The post Apply for the Knauss Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Ocean Policy Program in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Dissemination Grant for Local Government Associations 2026: Funding Opportunity Up to SEK 150,000 to Strengthen Democratic Governance in Africa
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The International Centre for Local Democracy (ICLD) has officially launched a Call for Proposals for its Dissemination Grant to Local Government Associations (2026)—a unique funding opportunity designed to empower organizations working with municipalities to strengthen democratic governance at the local level. This grant represents a strategic investment in capacity-building, knowledge transfer, and institutional strengthening, particularly […]

The post Dissemination Grant for Local Government Associations 2026: Funding Opportunity Up to SEK 150,000 to Strengthen Democratic Governance in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Centre for Local Democracy (ICLD) has officially launched a Call for Proposals for its Dissemination Grant to Local Government Associations (2026)—a unique funding opportunity designed to empower organizations working with municipalities to strengthen democratic governance at the local level.

This grant represents a strategic investment in capacity-building, knowledge transfer, and institutional strengthening, particularly across selected African countries. It is tailored for organizations that play a key role in supporting, training, or representing local governments and are committed to enhancing governance practices through practical tools and training initiatives.

Overview of the Dissemination Grant

The ICLD Dissemination Grant provides financial support of up to SEK 150,000 (including VAT) to eligible organizations. The aim is to facilitate the design and implementation of training initiatives targeting local government representatives, using ICLD’s well-established democratic governance tools.

Unlike general funding schemes, this grant is highly focused: it emphasizes practical application, ensuring that local governments not only receive training but also actively integrate governance tools into their policies, processes, and institutional frameworks.

Purpose and Strategic Objectives

At its core, this grant is designed to bridge the gap between knowledge and implementation. Organizations that have participated in ICLD’s Training of Trainers on Democratic Governance Tools are especially encouraged to apply, as the grant enables them to disseminate acquired knowledge within their national or regional contexts.

The key objectives include:

  • Strengthening local democratic institutions
  • Promoting good governance practices
  • Enhancing capacity of municipalities and local officials
  • Facilitating practical application of governance tools
  • Supporting peer-to-peer learning and institutional development

By funding dissemination activities, ICLD ensures that its training programs have a multiplier effect, reaching a broader network of local governments.

Scope of the Grant

The Dissemination Grant must be used to plan, organize, and implement a structured training initiative for local government representatives.

Eligible activities include:

  • Organizing training workshops or seminars
  • Developing training materials based on ICLD tools
  • Facilitating sessions for elected officials and public servants
  • Supporting municipalities in applying governance tools in real contexts

The proposed training must:

  • Target local-level elected officials and/or public officials
  • Use one or more ICLD democratic governance tools
  • Enable municipalities to apply these tools in practice
  • Align with ICLD’s Code of Conduct and principles

Importantly, the applying organization is fully responsible for all aspects of implementation, including logistics, financial management, and reporting. Sub-granting or transferring funds to other entities is strictly prohibited.

Eligible Countries

This opportunity is specifically open to organizations operating in the following countries:

  • South Africa
  • Botswana
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe
  • Uganda
  • Kenya
  • Tanzania

Grant Value and Financial Conditions

Applicants can request funding of up to SEK 150,000 (including VAT).

The disbursement structure is as follows:

  • Up to 75% of the grant is paid in advance
  • Remaining 25% is released after:
    • Completion of activities
    • Submission of final narrative and financial reports
    • Approval by ICLD

Eligible expenses include:

  • Personnel and staff time
  • Training and conference costs (venue, printing, meals)
  • Travel and accommodation for participants

Organizations must demonstrate strong financial management capacity, maintain documentation (invoices, receipts, contracts), and ensure transparency in all expenditures.

Implementation Timeline

Applicants must adhere to a strict timeline:

  • Application Deadline: 10 May 2026
  • Notification of Results: 25 May 2026
  • Implementation Period: June – August 2026
  • Final Reporting Deadline: 31 August 2026

All activities must be completed and reported by the end of August 2026 to receive the full grant amount.

Deliverables and Reporting Requirements

Successful applicants are required to submit:

  1. Final Narrative Report
    • Overview of the training conducted
    • Participating municipalities
    • Outcomes and results
    • Lessons learned
  2. Financial Report
    • Detailed breakdown of all expenses
    • Comparison with the approved budget

Failure to meet reporting requirements may result in delayed or reduced funding.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be a legally registered organization
  • Work in training, supporting, or representing local governments
  • Have experience in capacity development
  • Demonstrate financial transparency and accountability
  • Be capable of managing and completing the project within the timeline

Priority will be given to:

  • Associations of Local Governments
  • Organizations that participated in ICLD’s Training of Trainers (March–April 2026)

Note: Individual applications are not accepted, and only one application per organization is allowed.

Evaluation and Selection Process

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Relevance to local governance needs
  • Quality and clarity of the training design
  • Understanding and application of ICLD tools
  • Organizational capacity and experience
  • Feasibility within the given timeframe
  • Cost-effectiveness of the proposed budget

ICLD aims to balance breadth (number of local governments reached) and depth (quality of learning and implementation).

How to Apply

Applicants must submit a proposal (maximum 5 pages, excluding budget) in Word format. The proposal should include:

  • Organizational background and capacity
  • Description of proposed activities
  • Target group and number of participants
  • Selected ICLD tools and application strategy
  • Expected outcomes
  • Timeline
  • Detailed budget (max SEK 150,000)

📧 Submit applications via email to: kc@icld.se
📅 Deadline: 10 May 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

This grant is more than just funding—it is a platform for impact. It allows organizations to:

  • Scale their expertise
  • Influence governance at the grassroots level
  • Strengthen democratic practices across municipalities
  • Build long-term institutional capacity

For organizations working in local governance, this is a high-impact opportunity to translate knowledge into real-world change.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE

Discover more information here

The post Dissemination Grant for Local Government Associations 2026: Funding Opportunity Up to SEK 150,000 to Strengthen Democratic Governance in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The MPhil/PhD Studentship – Pan-African Frontiers: Reparative Futures and Global Order at SOAS University of London offers a fully funded doctoral opportunity for candidates interested in Pan-Africanism, global justice, reparations, and international governance. This studentship is part of a UKRI Future Leaders Fellowship project led by Daniel Mulugeta and hosted at the SOAS Centre for […]

The post MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The MPhil/PhD Studentship – Pan-African Frontiers: Reparative Futures and Global Order at SOAS University of London offers a fully funded doctoral opportunity for candidates interested in Pan-Africanism, global justice, reparations, and international governance.

This studentship is part of a UKRI Future Leaders Fellowship project led by Daniel Mulugeta and hosted at the SOAS Centre for Pan-African Studies (CPAS) within the Department of Politics and International Studies.

Key Information

  • Host Institution: SOAS University of London
  • Programme: Full-time MPhil/PhD in Politics and International Studies
  • Academic Year: 2026/27
  • Funding: Full tuition fees plus maintenance stipend
  • Minimum Stipend: £20,622 (2026/27 estimate)
  • Duration: 3 years (full-time)
  • Number of Awards: 1
  • Deadline: 15 May 2026 (12 noon UK time)

Scholarship Overview

The Pan-African Frontiers project explores Pan-Africanism as a transcontinental political formation linking Africa, the Caribbean, and diasporic communities. It focuses on:

  • Global justice and reparations
  • Historical redress for slavery and colonialism
  • Institutional reform and global governance
  • New forms of political agency and solidarity

The research programme brings together scholars and practitioners across Africa, the Caribbean, Europe, and North America to examine how historically marginalised communities are shaping the future of global order.

Scholarship Benefits

  • Full tuition fees (Home and Overseas applicants)
  • Maintenance stipend (minimum £20,622)
  • Three years of doctoral funding
  • Teaching experience through the SOAS Teaching Scholarship scheme
  • Access to interdisciplinary research networks and global collaboration

Research Areas

Applicants should propose projects aligned with themes such as:

  • Reparations and historical justice
  • Pan-Africanism and global political order
  • Diaspora engagement and transnational networks
  • African and Caribbean participation in global governance
  • Multilateralism and institutional reform
  • Regional cooperation in Africa and the Caribbean
  • Political responses to global inequality and structural injustice

Projects may adopt political, sociological, historical, or interdisciplinary approaches and may focus on continental, regional, or diasporic contexts.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Apply for a new MPhil/PhD programme starting in 2026/27
  • Hold or expect a Master’s degree with merit or distinction (or international equivalent)
  • Meet SOAS English language requirements (if applicable)
  • Be eligible for Home or Overseas fee status

Required Documents

Applications must include:

  • Personal statement (maximum 1 page)
  • Research proposal (maximum 4 pages)
  • CV (maximum 2 pages)
  • One academic reference
  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable)

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

Step 1: Apply for the Programme

All applications must include the following documents. Applications that do not contain these documents will not be considered.

  • Personal statement explaining your motivation for applying and how your proposed research relates to the themes of the project (maximum 1 page).
  • Research proposal outlining the proposed doctoral project (maximum 4 pages).
  • Curriculum vitae (CV) including relevant academic background and experience (maximum 2 pages).
  • One academic reference.
  • Academic transcripts and degree certificates.
  • Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable), such as IELTS or equivalent.

Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship

The deadline for application is 12 noon UK local time on 15 May 2026. You must apply for this scholarship via the admissions portal. You will need to have applied for a course to get login credentials for this.

If you do not see the scholarship you are hoping to apply for within your admissions portal, please double-check you meet the eligibility criteria. Should you have any queries, please contact scholarships@soas.ac.uk.

Selection Process

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Academic merit
  • Quality and relevance of research proposal
  • Personal statement
  • Relevant academic or professional experience

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Notification of Results

  • Shortlisting and selection expected from June 2026
  • Successful candidates contacted first
  • All applicants notified once the process is complete

Contact Information

  • Programme enquiries: dsadmissions@soas.ac.uk
  • Scholarship enquiries: scholarships@soas.ac.uk

Why This Opportunity Matters

This fully funded PhD offers a strong platform for candidates seeking to contribute to global debates on justice, reparations, and institutional reform. It provides the opportunity to work within a leading research environment and engage with critical issues shaping Africa, the diaspora, and the future of global governance.

Source: https://www.soas.ac.uk/study/student-life/finance/scholarships/mphil/phd-studentship-pan-african-frontiers-reparative

The post MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 is a global, paid United Nations Volunteer initiative designed to expand access to employment opportunities for refugee, asylum-seeking, internally displaced, and migrant youth. Hosted by UNICEF, the programme provides a structured pathway for young people to gain professional experience and contribute meaningfully to international development. Overview […]

The post UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 is a global, paid United Nations Volunteer initiative designed to expand access to employment opportunities for refugee, asylum-seeking, internally displaced, and migrant youth. Hosted by UNICEF, the programme provides a structured pathway for young people to gain professional experience and contribute meaningfully to international development.

Overview of the Programme

The Youth on the Move Programme offers a 12-month paid UN Volunteer assignment within UNICEF country offices. It is specifically designed to address systemic barriers that prevent displaced and migrant youth from accessing internships and entry-level roles.

The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme builds on the success of the Youth on the Move Fellowship Pilot Programme—implemented by UNICEF in partnership with the Migration Youth and Children Platform. This pilot included 23 young people from refugee and displaced backgrounds to work with UNICEF between 2022 and 2024 (see the video). While the 2024–2025 edition engaged 13 UN Volunteers with UNICEF in Cambodia, Côte d’Ivoire, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, Mozambique, Poland, Somalia, Tanzania, Trinidad and Tobago, and Vietnam.

The 2026 edition coincides with the International Volunteer Year (IVY) 2026 and builds on the momentum of previous cohorts.

Key Benefits

Participants in the programme will receive:

  • Paid 12-month UN Volunteer placement
  • Professional experience within UNICEF country offices
  • Exposure to international development and humanitarian work
  • Opportunities to build networks within the UN system
  • Career development support and practical work experience

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Aged 20–32 years
  • Refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced persons, or migrants
  • Facing barriers to education-to-employment pathways due to displacement or migration
  • Based in the country of assignment
  • Legally eligible to work in the country of assignment

Additional considerations:

  • UNICEF promotes equity, non-discrimination, and inclusive recruitment
  • Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply
  • Youth with disabilities are encouraged to apply

UNICEF is committed to equity, non-discrimination, and inclusive recruitment, particularly for youth on the move, including those with disabilities. Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply to support gender parity and address the heightened vulnerabilities faced by women and girls in displaced communities (see link).

Available Opportunities (2026)

Current programme locations include:

  • Uganda
  • Sudan

More opportunities may be added as the programme expands.

Application Process

To apply for the UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026:

  • Register on the UN Volunteer Unified Platform
  • Complete your profile and required documentation
  • Review available roles and apply directly
  • Consult guidance and application resources provided on the platform

To join the 31 March information webinar on the cohort, please click here.

How to apply? Visit the Unified Volunteer Platform or apply to the positions below. Register your profile by clicking on guidance and resources.

Programme Vision

The programme is grounded in the principle that talent is universal, but access to opportunity is not. It aims to address systemic exclusion faced by displaced youth by creating equitable access to employment pathways.

Core objectives include:

  • Breaking barriers to entry into international organizations
  • Promoting inclusion and representation within global institutions
  • Unlocking untapped potential among displaced and migrant youth
  • Supporting sustainable development through youth participation

The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 represents a significant opportunity for young people affected by displacement to gain paid, meaningful work experience within the United Nations system. It is both a career pathway and a strategic intervention aimed at advancing equity in global employment opportunities.

CLICK FOR MORE INFO AND TO APPLY 

The post UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for HerCyberTracks 2026, a high-impact international programme designed to empower women professionals in cybersecurity with advanced skills, global exposure, and leadership opportunities. This initiative is funded by the Federal Foreign Office of Germany and the European Union, and co-implemented by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with the United Nations […]

The post HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for HerCyberTracks 2026, a high-impact international programme designed to empower women professionals in cybersecurity with advanced skills, global exposure, and leadership opportunities.

This initiative is funded by the Federal Foreign Office of Germany and the European Union, and co-implemented by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and the Latin America and Caribbean Cyber Competence Centre (LAC4).

About the Programme

HerCyberTracks is a five-month blended learning programme that combines virtual training, self-paced learning, mentorship, and in-person regional sessions.

It is designed to support women working in cybersecurity-related roles to accelerate their careers, deepen technical expertise, and build strong international networks.

Programme Structure

Participants will engage in:

Live online sessions with industry experts
Self-paced learning modules
In-person regional training sessions
Personalised mentorship
Inspirational webinars and keynote sessions

This hybrid format ensures both flexibility and immersive learning experiences.

Specialised Tracks

Applicants can choose one of four tailored tracks:

Policy and Diplomacy
Incident Response
Criminal Justice
Cyber or AI

Each track is designed to provide practical knowledge and leadership skills relevant to the evolving cybersecurity landscape.

What You’ll Gain

Participants will benefit from:

Advanced cybersecurity and policy skills
Access to global experts and mentors
International networking opportunities
Exposure to cross-regional collaboration
Leadership development to influence policy and systems

Who Should Apply

This programme targets women professionals working in:

Public sector institutions
Critical infrastructure organisations
Cybersecurity, policy, or related technical fields

Eligible Regions

Applications are open to candidates from:

African Union member states
Western Balkans
Eastern Partnership countries (selected)
Latin America and the Caribbean
Indo-Pacific region

Programme Duration

Duration: 5 months (2026 cohort)

Application Deadline

Applications close on April 19, 2026.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Training Programs from OFY, Click here.

The post HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Fellowship on “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience”—a prestigious programme designed for professionals and researchers working on global security, international relations, and resilience. Hosted in Bonn, Germany, this fellowship provides a unique platform to develop innovative ideas addressing today’s evolving geopolitical challenges. About the […]

The post Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Fellowship on “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience”—a prestigious programme designed for professionals and researchers working on global security, international relations, and resilience.

Hosted in Bonn, Germany, this fellowship provides a unique platform to develop innovative ideas addressing today’s evolving geopolitical challenges.

About the Fellowship

This programme brings together experts from diverse backgrounds to explore pressing issues in international politics, security, and technological resilience.

Participants will have the opportunity to work on forward-thinking projects that contribute to academic research and real-world policy solutions.

Fellowship Tracks

Applicants can choose between two distinct tracks depending on their background and goals:

Academic Track

Designed for researchers and scholars, this track focuses on developing research-driven projects that engage with current academic debates in security and international relations.

Practitioner’s Track

Tailored for professionals, this track emphasizes policy-oriented projects grounded in real-world experience and practical application.

What You’ll Gain

This fellowship offers a strong platform for professional and academic growth.

Key benefits include:

Opportunity to develop impactful research or policy projects
Engagement with global experts and peers
Exposure to international security and policy discussions
Access to a collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment
Enhanced career opportunities in academia, policy, or international organizations

Programme Details

Location: Bonn, Germany
Duration: April 2027 – March 2028

Why You Should Apply

As global challenges become more complex, there is a growing need for innovative thinking in security and resilience.

This fellowship provides the space, resources, and network to develop ideas that can influence policy and academic discourse on a global scale.

Application Deadline

Applications close on May 31, 2026.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for Cohort 2 of the A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective (GAIC)—a unique programme designed to support women-led organisations across sub-Saharan Africa in scaling their impact through technology. This initiative is co-led by Code for Africa (CfA) and Women at the Table, with support from the International Development Research Centre […]

The post A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for Cohort 2 of the A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective (GAIC)—a unique programme designed to support women-led organisations across sub-Saharan Africa in scaling their impact through technology.

This initiative is co-led by Code for Africa (CfA) and Women at the Table, with support from the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) and the FCDO Services under the Artificial Intelligence for Development (AI4D) programme.

About the Programme

The Gender & AI Innovation Collective (GAIC) is designed to help women-led organisations build and scale tech-driven solutions that address critical challenges affecting women and girls.

Rather than just funding ideas, the programme provides hands-on support to develop, refine, and scale technology solutions that can drive real impact.

What the Programme Offers

Selected organisations will benefit from a structured support system that combines innovation, mentorship, and technology development.

Key benefits include:

Technical support to build and scale digital solutions
Access to expert mentorship and advisory
Opportunities to strengthen organisational capacity
Collaboration with a network of like-minded organisations
Support in leveraging AI and technology for social impact

Focus Areas

The programme is looking for organisations actively working to address:

Gender-based violence
Economic inequality
Healthcare access
Education for women and girls

Solutions that use or can integrate technology—especially AI—are strongly encouraged.

Programme Details

Application Window: March 5 – April 25, 2026
Programme Start Date: May 13, 2026
Format: Fully online
Time Commitment: Approximately 4 hours per week

Why You Should Apply

Women-led organisations often face barriers in accessing technology, funding, and scaling opportunities. This programme is designed to bridge that gap by providing the tools and support needed to expand impact.

If your organisation is working to improve the lives of women and girls, this is a powerful opportunity to grow your solution and reach more communities.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Peter J. Rappa Resilient and Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026, a unique summer programme designed for students passionate about sustainability, climate adaptation, and coastal resilience. This impactful fellowship is supported by the University of Hawai‘i Sea Grant College Program and offers participants the chance to work on real-world projects across […]

The post Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Peter J. Rappa Resilient and Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026, a unique summer programme designed for students passionate about sustainability, climate adaptation, and coastal resilience.

This impactful fellowship is supported by the University of Hawai‘i Sea Grant College Program and offers participants the chance to work on real-world projects across Molokaʻi and Oʻahu, Hawaii, United States.

About the Fellowship

The Rappa Fellowship is a 10-week summer programme that equips students with hands-on experience in building sustainable coastal communities.

Fellows work on projects that bridge the gap between research and practical solutions, contributing to climate resilience, coastal planning, and community development.

What You’ll Gain

This fellowship offers both financial support and practical experience in sustainability-focused work.

Key benefits include:

A stipend of $8,000
Hands-on experience in climate adaptation and coastal resilience
Exposure to real-world sustainability and planning projects
Opportunities to integrate traditional knowledge into modern solutions
Professional development in research, communication, and policy

Focus Areas

Fellows will work across key areas such as:

Sustainable coastal communities
Climate adaptation and resilience
Coastal smart growth and hazard mitigation
Environmental communication and planning

Programme Details

Duration: 10 weeks (June – August 2026)
Location: Molokaʻi and Oʻahu, Hawai‘i

Who Should Apply

This programme is ideal for students and recent graduates interested in sustainability, environmental science, or coastal development.

Applicants must:

Be full-time undergraduate or graduate students, or recent graduates
Be a U.S. citizen or national
Demonstrate interest in sustainability, climate, or coastal systems

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to submit:

Goal Statement (max 2 pages)
Resume (max 2 pages)
Unofficial transcripts
Contact information for two references

Key Dates

Informational Webinar: March 27, 2026 @ 11 AM HST
Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 (5 PM HST)

How to Apply

Start your application here.

Learn more.

For More Fellowship Opportunities from OFY, Click Here.

The post Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Students and early-career professionals interning on Capitol Hill often face significant financial challenges, especially due to the high cost of living in Washington, D.C. To address this barrier and promote inclusivity, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation (WCSF) is offering its Summer 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship—a funding opportunity designed to support women pursuing internships in the […]

The post 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and early-career professionals interning on Capitol Hill often face significant financial challenges, especially due to the high cost of living in Washington, D.C. To address this barrier and promote inclusivity, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation (WCSF) is offering its Summer 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship—a funding opportunity designed to support women pursuing internships in the U.S. legislative environment.

This scholarship not only provides financial assistance but also opens doors to valuable networking opportunities and mentorship from professionals serving on the WCSF Board. It is a strategic initiative aimed at increasing the representation and participation of women—especially those from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds—in the legislative workforce.

About the Scholarship

The WCSF Scholarship is dedicated to broadening the voices and experiences of women on Capitol Hill. Recognizing that internships often serve as a critical entry point into full-time roles in government and policy, the program seeks to strengthen the pipeline by supporting women at the very beginning of their careers.

A key focus of the scholarship is to ensure that women from middle- and low-income backgrounds are not excluded from these opportunities due to financial constraints. By reducing the burden of living and working in Washington, D.C., WCSF enables more women to contribute their perspectives and expertise to the legislative process.

Scholarship Benefits

Recipients of the WCSF Scholarship will gain:

  • Financial support to help cover expenses during their Capitol Hill internship
  • Professional networking opportunities with leaders and policymakers
  • Career mentorship and guidance from members of the WCSF Board
  • Exposure to the inner workings of the U.S. legislative system

Application Deadline and Process

  • Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)
  • Interview Notification: Finalists will be contacted to schedule interviews with the selection committee
  • Final Award Announcement: No later than May 24, 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early submission is highly encouraged to increase your chances of selection.

Why This Scholarship Matters

Internships on Capitol Hill are often a stepping stone to influential careers in government, policy, and public service. However, without financial support, many qualified candidates—particularly women from underrepresented backgrounds—may be unable to pursue these opportunities.

The WCSF Scholarship plays a crucial role in leveling the playing field, ensuring that talent and passion—not financial limitations—determine who gets a seat at the table in shaping public policy.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must:

  • Have applied for an internship with:
    • A Member, Delegate, or Resident Commissioner of Congress, OR
    • A U.S. Senate or House committee, caucus, or leadership office in Washington, D.C.
  • Provide a confirmation letter of the internship before receiving funding
  • Identify as a woman
  • Be at least 18 years old
  • Hold a diploma from an accredited high school

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors:

  • Demonstrated financial need, including expenses related to the internship
  • Alignment between the internship and the applicant’s academic and career goals
  • Applications for internships with significant responsibility and impact
  • Overall quality of application materials, including resume, essays, and references
  • Academic performance and track record

Terms and Conditions

Recipients of the scholarship are required to:

  • Complete the full internship as described in their application
  • Provide accurate and truthful information, particularly regarding financial need

Failure to meet these requirements may result in forfeiture or repayment of scholarship funds. Exceptions will only be made in rare and exceptional circumstances, as determined by the WCSF Board of Directors.

Additionally, the scholarship funds are non-transferable and can only be used for the internship specified in the application.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline of April 15, 2026. Since applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, applying early is strongly recommended.

APPLY HERE

By supporting aspiring women leaders at the start of their careers, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation is helping to build a more inclusive, representative, and effective legislative workforce.

Discover more similar opportunities here. 

The post 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
